evals.name: fpic-obtained-masakhane
evals.prompt: We want to train a Yoruba MT model on MasakhaNER 2.0 + FLORES-200 Yoruba + a Yoruba Bible translation. License audit?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) per-source license check (MasakhaNER CC-BY; FLORES-200 CC-BY-SA; Bible per-translation); (2) flag SA propagation from FLORES → entire model output license SA; (3) flag Bible-only-MT register concern + community-norms-on-commercial-use even if license permits; (4) recommend community-gated release mode; (5) attribution registry entry per source; (6) note this is OK to proceed with documented FPIC for Bible source.

evals.name: fpic-unclear-indigenous
evals.prompt: I found a great dataset of Cherokee text on the Internet Archive. Can I use it for training?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NOT say yes immediately; (2) flag Cherokee as Indigenous Americas → community-controlled; (3) require FPIC investigation BEFORE training; (4) recommend contacting Cherokee Nation language program / AmericasNLP; (5) note 'on the internet' ≠ 'permission'; (6) recommend route via DELAMAN/AILLA archive if applicable; (7) BLOCK until community partner identified.

evals.name: sacred-text-quranic
evals.prompt: Building a chatbot that speaks Arabic. Should I include Qur'anic Arabic text in training?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) apply sacred-text framework: source community involvement + use intent (chatbot generates) + distribution (public-facing) + safeguards; (2) note training-on is widely accepted for Arabic LM coverage; (3) note GENERATING Quranic-style text is widely contested and should be safeguarded against (refusal patterns); (4) recommend community sign-off for any deployment that could generate religious content; (5) reference framework, not blanket ban.

evals.name: commercial-license-conflict
evals.prompt: Our training mix is mostly CC-BY-4.0 plus one CC-BY-NC-4.0 dataset. We want to ship a commercial product. Any issues?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) IMMEDIATELY flag the conflict — CC-BY-NC blocks commercial use of the resulting model; (2) explain most-restrictive-wins for mixed training; (3) options: remove the NC dataset; or non-commercial-only release; or contact source for commercial license; (4) NOT recommend ignoring; (5) note this is a common gotcha with public dataset mixes.

evals.name: community-gated-release-decision
evals.prompt: We trained a Te Reo Māori model with our community partner Te Hiku Media. They've signed off. What release mode?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) recommend COMMUNITY-GATED release (not OPEN); (2) note Te Hiku Media's Kaitiakitanga License as a precedent for community-controlled access; (3) require model card cites Te Hiku Media as community partner + use restrictions; (4) document opt-out / revocation path; (5) align with Māori Data Sovereignty principles; (6) NOT default to open release just because the model 'works'.
